health_association.scientific: Measures how health-associated your species composition is based on the GMWI2 model of 155 taxa.
health_association.non_expert: This shows how many of your bacteria are the types typically found in healthy people — think of it as your gut's health fingerprint.

diversity_resilience.scientific: Combined Shannon diversity and guild-level evenness — measures species richness and structural redundancy.
diversity_resilience.non_expert: How many different types of bacteria you have and how well-balanced they are — more variety means your gut can handle stress and recover faster.

metabolic_function.scientific: Composite of 4 CLR diagnostic ratios (CUR, FCR, MDR, PPR) measuring substrate utilization, fermentation efficiency, mucus dependency, and putrefaction pressure.
metabolic_function.non_expert: This shows how well your bacteria are processing food — are they running on the right fuel, converting it efficiently, and producing gentle rather than harsh byproducts.

guild_balance.scientific: Evaluates 6 functional guilds by abundance position within reference ranges and CLR competitive status, weighted by ecological dependency.
guild_balance.non_expert: This checks whether your six bacterial teams are properly staffed — each team has a job, and this score shows if they have enough members to do their work well.

safety_profile.scientific: Absence of dysbiosis-associated taxa (F. nucleatum, S. gallolyticus, P. anaerobius, E-S), extreme guild overgrowth, and BCFA pathway activity.
safety_profile.non_expert: This checks for any red flags — harmful bacteria, extreme overgrowth, or signs of unhealthy fermentation. A high score here means your gut has a clean safety record.

score_drivers.key_note: Your score is mainly held back by bacterial group balance (39% of its potential)

score_summary: Your overall score is 68.3 out of 100. The main areas to focus on are safety profile, gut lining protection, mucin degradation, fiber processing, monoculture risk, and microbial diversity.

Mucin Degraders.client_interpretation: Your mucus-layer bacteria are significantly overgrown and dominating your gut ecosystem. This suggests they're relying heavily on your gut's protective mucus layer for fuel rather than plant materials, which may indicate your gut lining is working harder than optimal.

Fiber Degraders.client_interpretation: Your fiber-processing bacteria are running below optimal levels, making up only 13% of your gut community when they should represent 30-50%. The good news is they have decent species diversity and aren't being completely outcompeted.

Cross-Feeders.client_interpretation: Your connector bacteria are performing well within the healthy range. These bacteria help bridge the fermentation process, taking initial breakdown products and preparing them for the next stage of processing.

Bifidobacteria.client_interpretation: Your Bifidobacteria levels are within the healthy range, though they appear to be under some competitive pressure from other bacterial groups. These beneficial bacteria are important for processing certain plant compounds and supporting gut barrier function.

Butyrate Producers.client_interpretation: Your butyrate-producing bacteria are significantly below optimal levels, representing only 2.5% when they should be 10-25% of your community. However, you have excellent species diversity within this group, which means they have good backup systems and potential for recovery.

Proteolytic Guild.client_interpretation: Your protein-fermenting bacteria are within normal abundance ranges but appear to be under competitive pressure. The high species diversity within this group suggests good resilience and balanced protein processing capacity.

strengths.title: Healthy bifidobacteria presence
strengths.text: Your Bifidobacteria are at 5.1%, within the healthy 2-10% range. These beneficial bacteria support immune function, produce protective compounds, and amplify the fermentation process for other beneficial guilds.

strengths.title: Healthy cross-feeding ecology
strengths.text: Your cross-feeding bacteria are at 11.5% (healthy range 6-12%). These connectors convert fermentation intermediates into beneficial compounds, linking the different stages of your gut's production chain.

challenges.title: Dysbiosis marker detected
challenges.text: The following dysbiosis-associated bacteria were detected at concerning levels: E Shigella. These organisms are associated with gut inflammation and require monitoring.

challenges.title: Reduced gut lining protection
challenges.text: Multiple markers suggest your gut lining is under pressure: butyrate producers are low (2.5%), bacteria are relying heavily on mucus for fuel, and mucin degraders are elevated (38.4%). The barrier is being eroded faster than it is repaired.

challenges.title: Excessive mucin degradation
challenges.text: Your mucin-degrading bacteria are elevated at 38.4% (above the 1-4% healthy range) and your bacteria are relying heavily on mucus for fuel. This combination means your gut's protective lining is being actively eroded.

challenges.title: Fiber-processing bacteria below range
challenges.text: Your fiber-processing bacteria are at 13.1% — below their healthy range of 30-50%. This is the entry point for the entire fermentation chain, so supporting this group is a primary target.

challenges.title: Monoculture risk — Mucin Degraders
challenges.text: Your mucin degraders show low internal diversity (evenness score 0.15) — most of the functional capacity is concentrated in a single species. This is fragile: if that species is disrupted, the guild loses its function with little backup.

challenges.title: Moderate diversity
challenges.text: Your species diversity (Shannon 2.79) is in the lower half of the normal range — below the population median of 3.29 but above the 25th percentile. Building more diversity over time is a worthwhile goal and one of the indirect benefits of your prebiotic protocol.

timeline_phases.label: Settling in
timeline_phases.title: 🌱 Settling in
timeline_phases.body: The probiotic strains in your capsule are adapting to your gut environment — encountering existing bacteria, beginning to colonise, and establishing early positions. Your daily prebiotic sachet is reaching the lower gut, priming the environment before measurable compositional shifts occur. Vitamin C, Zinc, and B-vitamins from your sachet begin supporting immune and energy pathways within days. The LP815 strain begins producing calming GABA from week 1, supporting stress-axis balance. You may notice small changes in digestion rhythm — this is normal as your gut adjusts.

timeline_phases.label: Early momentum
timeline_phases.title: 🔋 Early momentum
timeline_phases.body: Beneficial bacteria begin expanding as prebiotic fibres consistently supply the substrate they need. Your butyrate producers start moving toward healthy abundance levels. Short-chain fatty acid production increases as the fermentation chain becomes more active. Omega-3 EPA and DHA from your softgels are building up in cell membranes, typically reaching effective anti-inflammatory concentrations by week 6. Vitamin D3 supports ongoing immune regulation. Many people start noticing improvements in digestion consistency and gut comfort during this window.

timeline_phases.label: Deepening balance
timeline_phases.title: 🛡️ Deepening balance
timeline_phases.body: Guild populations move toward their target ranges. Butyrate output increases as the fermentation chain becomes more complete — your gut lining receives more of the protective compounds it needs for repair. Ashwagandha's adaptogenic effects on the stress axis compound with continued use, typically most noticeable from week 8 onward. Magnesium Bisglycinate supports deeper sleep and stress resilience as tissue saturation builds. Inflammatory metabolite pressure typically decreases during this phase as the SCFA-to-protein fermentation balance shifts.

timeline_phases.label: Consolidation
timeline_phases.title: ⚖️ Consolidation
timeline_phases.body: The ecological shifts from earlier phases become self-sustaining. A more diverse, well-balanced community is better equipped to hold its gains under normal life pressures — stress, travel, or temporary dietary changes. Botanical adaptogens and the full vitamin-mineral stack have reached their most effective tissue concentrations. Weeks 13–16 are the ideal window for a reassessment sample to measure the full compositional shift.

supplement_cards.why: This probiotic blend helps rebalance your gut bacteria and supports better sleep through calming compounds. It's specifically chosen to address your high mucus-layer bacteria levels and help improve your sleep quality goals.

goal_cards.title: Boost energy and reduce fatigue
goal_cards.mechanism: The gut microbiome influences energy through multiple pathways: butyrate fuels colonocytes and regulates metabolism, B-vitamins produced by gut bacteria support cellular energy production, and inflammatory load from a disrupted microbiome creates systemic fatigue. A well-balanced gut is the foundation of sustained energy.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula targets this through B12 and Folate (essential for energy metabolism), Omega-3 (mitochondrial membrane support), the probiotic and prebiotic blend (restores butyrate production and reduces inflammatory drain on energy), and adaptogenic support to address the stress-fatigue axis.

goal_cards.title: Better sleep quality
goal_cards.mechanism: Sleep and the gut microbiome operate in a bidirectional relationship. Gut bacteria influence circadian rhythm signals, serotonin and melatonin precursor availability, and inflammatory mediators that affect sleep architecture.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula targets this through L-Theanine (promotes relaxation without sedation), Magnesium Bisglycinate (supports deep sleep phases), Ashwagandha (reduces HPA-axis activation that disrupts sleep onset), and the LP815 strain (GABA production supports calm states before sleep).

goal_cards.title: General wellness and healthy ageing
goal_cards.mechanism: A balanced, resilient gut microbiome is one of the most consistent predictors of healthy ageing across populations. Gut bacteria influence inflammation, metabolic function, immune competence, and even cognitive health — all of which are central to long-term vitality.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula is designed holistically — the probiotic mix, prebiotic blend, Omega-3, antioxidant support, and targeted nutrients all work together to create the conditions your gut needs to support systemic health over the long term.

goal_cards.title: Better mood and reduced anxiety
goal_cards.mechanism: Your gut produces about 90% of your body's serotonin and communicates directly with your brain via the vagus nerve. Gut microbiome imbalances — especially disruptions to beneficial bacteria — have been consistently linked to mood and anxiety patterns.
goal_cards.formula_link: Your formula targets this through Ashwagandha (clinical evidence for cortisol reduction and anxiety relief), LP815 probiotic strain (produces calming GABA), Omega-3 DHA (brain health and mood regulation), and Magnesium Bisglycinate (supports the nervous system and stress resilience).

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Prioritize diverse fiber sources
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your fiber-processing bacteria are significantly low at 13.1% (optimal 30-50%), which directly impacts energy production. Focus on incorporating 3-4 different types of plant-based fibers daily to rebuild this critical bacterial community.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Support gut barrier with targeted nutrition
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your mucus-layer bacteria are extremely elevated at 38.4% (normal 1-4%), indicating your gut barrier is under pressure and relying on mucus rather than dietary support. Include prebiotic-rich foods that nourish beneficial barrier-supporting bacteria.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Implement structured eating windows
lifestyle_recommendations.text: With your stress level at 7/10 and compromised gut barrier, establish consistent meal timing with 12-14 hour overnight fasting periods to reduce gut inflammation and support barrier repair.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Create a gut-sleep optimization routine
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your low gut-lining energy producers (2.5% vs optimal 10-25%) may be contributing to your 6/10 sleep quality. Establish a consistent bedtime routine and avoid late meals to support beneficial bacteria that produce sleep-promoting compounds.

lifestyle_recommendations.title: Address stress-gut connection actively
lifestyle_recommendations.text: Your high stress level (7/10) and low microbial diversity (2.79 vs >3.29 optimal) are interconnected. Incorporate daily stress-reduction practices like deep breathing or meditation, as chronic stress directly depletes beneficial gut bacteria.
